item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i found a few weird grammatical errors and i'm not sure if they were tone-intentional or not.

+ proofreading spoiler block
- again these may be tonal
 Phase 3: SCP-5103’s primary temporal compression mechanism is activated, it cannot be stopped for a seemingly random duration.

this sentence doesn't parse correctly. possible missing word? 
 Of particular note is the apparent presence of “pattern screamers”, first identified by personnel assigned to SCP-3930.

this needs a crosslink. 
 Requests: -Production and experimentation of SRSs (Approved) -Conduct research to identify SCP-5103's energy source -(Approved)

i feel like that the content of the third bullet should be at the end of the second bullet. 
 Junior Researcher Vera Calden was presently supervising during the initial exploration,

that should be "present supervising" if i'm not mistaken. 
 I supposes it is quite agonizing to trapped between dead realities,

that should probably just be "suppose".

none of this takes away from the article as a whole, of course. absolute +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm feeling a heavy novote because this sets out to be a universal SCP-2000, and does it well, but it's crippled by an absence of character. Calden is obviously in the spotlight but I never get a sense for who they are beyond the circumstances they're thrust into.

Also I'm not sure fragment pages are meant to be used like that; you may want to use a ListPages module to display them as offsets or make them supplement pages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so fucking cool. Reality failure scenarios are way more interesting than just massive destruction. Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get it. There's something about maintaining the strength of reality, by sucking raw reality out of another universe, like a huge SRA? And our own SRAs are using up its power? I dunno, that's about all I was able to understand.

But. I was enraptured the whole time. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Figured I might as well get this one out there. Took forever, but hey, I'm satisfied with the results.

The image I used was edited by myself, with the original image being here. Pixabay is copyleft, free use, and the like.

Thanks to Dr Moned for helping me flesh out the idea in IRC! Thanks to HammerMaiden, JakdragonX, and JustBixby for critting my longest skip yet! I'm probably missing a few other people, so, thanks to all of you guys as well.

Finally, thanks to you readers for allowing me to bask in the glory of effort not spent in vain!
